Mi Zhu (Zizhong)
麋竺 (子仲)

Mi Zhu, styledZizhong, was fromQu[朐],Donghai[東海].
His ancestors had been merchants for generations. They had servants and retainers in the tens of thousands, and their wealth and estate was in the hundreds ofthousands.A
TheRecords of the Search for Spiritsstates:[Mi] Zhuwas once returning fromLuo[洛] and had not even arrived within tens of miles from his home when he saw a woman on the side of the road. She requested to be picked up by[Mi] Zhu. They travelled several miles when the woman thanked her and left. She told[Mi] Zhu, “I am a messenger from Heaven. I am going to burn the house ofMi ZhuofDonghai[東海], but I am so touched that you, sir, picked me up that I am telling you this.”[Mi] Zhutherefore privately beseeched her. The woman said, “I cannot leave it unburnt. This being the case, you should hurry there and I will move slowly. In the middle of the day, a fire will break out.”[Mi] Zhureturned to his home and hurriedly took out his wealth and objects. In the middle of the day, a fire fiercely broke out.
Later, theGovernorofXu province[徐州牧],Tao Qian, recruited him to serve asAssistant OfficialClerk. When[Tao] Qiandied,[Mi] Zhureceived the last orders of[Tao] Qianto welcome theFormer LordatXiaopei[小沛].
In the first year of theera [196],Lü Buexploited the departure of theFormer Lord, who was resisting[Yuan] Shu, to attackXiapi[下邳], seizing the wife and children of theFormer Lord. TheFormer Lordturned his army fromHaixi[海西] inGuangling[廣陵]. Hence,[Mi] Zhugave hissisterto theFormer Lordas a wife, two thousand slaves and retainers, and valuables such as gold and silver to help the finances of the army. At the time, they were poor and lacking, so they were reliant on such donations.
Later,Excellency Caopetitioned for[Mi] Zhuto serve asGrand AdministratorofYing commandery[嬴郡太守]Band for the younger brother of[Mi] Zhu,[Mi] Fang, to be theChancellorofPengcheng[彭城相], but they all left their offices and followed the Former Lord in his travels. When theFormer Lordwas going toJing province[荊州], he sent[Mi] Zhuahead to exchange information withLiu Biao.[Mi] Zhuwas madeGentleman AttendantunderGeneral of the Left. WhenYi province[益州] was pacified, he was made to serve asGeneral who Secures the Han, being ranked superior to theGeneral who Directs the Military.
TheCollection of Excellency Caocontains the petition of theExcellency, which states:
“The area ofTaishan commandery[泰山郡] is vast and extensive, but it has been impudent and violent many times in the past. As a expedient and timely measure, five counties ought to be partitioned to formYing commandery[嬴郡]. Those who are pure and incorrupt should chosen to act as guarding generals.Deputy GeneralMi Zhuhas always acted loyally and purely, and his civil and martial skills manifest themselves prominently. I ask for[Mi] Zhuto be madeGrand AdministratorofYing commandery[嬴郡太守] so as to comfort and ease the officials and people.”
[Mi] Zhuwas gentle in demeanor and he was honest and sincere, but his directing ability was not the best. This is why he was treated with the etiquette of a eminent guest, but never held leadership. However, in terms of rewards and preferential treatment, there were none that compared to his.
[Mi] Fangserved asGrand AdministratorofNan commandery[南郡太守] and handled affairs together withGuan Yu. However, he had private interests and treacherous thoughts, and rebelled to receiveSun Quan, which led to the defeat of[Guan] Yu.[Mi] Zhutied himself up and asked to be punished, but theFormer Lordcomforted him on the account that the crimes of brothers do not implicate the other and gave him honorary treatment like before. But[Mi] Zhufelt so ashamed and depressed he fell ill and died after several years.
His son was[Mi] Wei, who attained the rank ofHousehold General of the Rapid Tigers. The son of[Mi] Weiwas[Mi] Zhao, who was Supervisor of the Tiger Cavalry. From[Mi] Zhuto[Mi] Zhao, they were all good at archery and horse riding, being proficient in shooting [arrows] and controlling [their horse].
translations.page_title: Mi Zhu (Zizhong) 麋竺 (子仲) Sanguozhi Biography by ZL181 - Records of the Three Kingdoms (三國志) by Chen Shou – Kongming’s Archives

Mi Zhu, styled Zizhong, was from Ju in Donghai. For generations his family engaged in commerce, and amassed more than ten thousand servants and retainers, and a fortune measured in hundreds ofmillions.A
Shoushen Ji: Once, Mi Zhu was coming home from Luoyang, and was several tens of li away from home, when he saw a woman standing by the road asking for a lift. After traveling for some li together, the woman thanked Mi Zhu and said to Zhu before leaving, “I am a messenger from Heaven, sent to burn down the home of Mi Zhu of Dong Hai. Since you have been so kind to give me a lift, I let you know about it.” Mi Zhu then pleaded with her. “I can’t but burn it,” she said. “Alright, why don’t we do this: You sir, speed along home. I will walk slowly there, but at midday the fire shall start.” And so Mi Zhu hurried home, and took his possessions to a safe place. At midday, indeed, the house was set ablaze.
At some point in time, Governor Tiao Qian of Xuzhou raised him from a commoner to being an Aide-de-Camp. When Tiao Qian died, Mi Zhu followed his last wish and took the First Ruler [Liu Bei] in [as the new governor].
In the first year of Jian’an (AD 196), Lü Bu took advantage of Liu Bei being out at war with Yuan Shu, and sneak attacked Xiapi, taking the First Ruler’s family captive. And so the First Ruler had to turn to go to Haixi in Guangling. At that time, Mi Zhu gave his younger sister in marriage to the First Ruler, and presented two thousand servants and various amounts of gold and money to help support [Liu Bei’s] army. Liu Bei could not have recovered from his losses were it not for Mi Zhu’s help.
Some time after that, the Lord Cao Cao memorialized to have Mi Zhu made Grand Administrator of Yin Commandery,Band Mi Zhu’s younger brother, Mi Fang, made prefect of Pangcheng. However, both of them declined the positions, but followed the First Ruler around instead. Right before the First Ruler went to take refuge in Jingzhou, he sent Mi Zhu in first to make an introduction to Liu Biao. Mi Zhu was then made Aide to the General of the Left. Once Yizhou was conquered, [Liu Bei] bestowed upon Mi Zhu the title “General of the Peace of Han”, and a rank second only to the General Master of the Army.
The lord’s memorial is found in the Anthology of Lord Cao as thus: “The border of Taishan Commandery is long and the land is vast, and there has been many bandits. For the time being, the best thing to do is to separate five counties out to make a Ying Commandery, and select an honest and upright man to be guard over it. Now, Lieutenant-General Mi Zhu has been known for being loyal and trustworthy, and he is renowned for both civil and military abilities. I petition to have Mi Zhu take the position of the Grand Administrator of Yin Commandery, so to please the officers and bring peace to the people.”
Mi Zhu was graceful, poised, and eloquent, but to propose strategy or deal with the affairs of the kingdom was not his specialty. That is why although he was treated with the utmost respect and honor, he was never put in command of anything. Nonetheless, none could compare to the lavish favors that were bestowed on him.
